description: M4 golden-scene visual-equivalence corpus (ROADMAP §1 M4 exit; issue #141): each corpus scene is rendered offscreen per backend and gated against the committed baseline with the named SSIM-class metric below. 'Identical desktop+web' = the same T1 semantics within per-scene tolerances, NOT bit-identical frames (float->unorm rounding legally differs per backend).

metric.name: mean block-SSIM
metric.note: SSIM computed per 8x8 non-overlapping Rec.709-luma window, averaged; 1.0 = identical.

rebaseline_policy: Baselines are committed, review-gated artifacts (ROADMAP §1 M4 exit: rebaselines are REVIEWED changes, never automatic). To rebaseline, replace the goldens/*.ppm in a PR that names the intentional visual change; never regenerate silently. Reference renders: `context_render_wgpu_offscreen golden <scene> <out.ppm>` (native) / tools/web_golden_run.py (browser).
blocking_policy: Minimal v1 (M4 exit ruling): Linux-Vulkan (lavapipe, CI render job) + one browser (headless Chromium + SwiftShader, CI render-web job) are BLOCKING; other backends are advisory until their R-QA-012 runner rows are provisioned.

triangle3d.description: The 3D render-pipeline scene: clear + one clip-space triangle through the T1 RHI. Flat colors — the M0 spike measured byte-identical frames through Naga (native) and Tint (browser), so the tolerance is tight.

sprite2d.description: The 2D scene: ortho-projected overlapping sprites with sorting-layer overdraw. Flat colors — tight tolerance.

lit3d.renderer: golden_lit.h::render_golden_lit3d (lit_offscreen.h base config: PBR + directional/point lights + shadow depth pass)
lit3d.description: The representative T1 scene: World -> extract -> shadow pass + PBR main pass. Gradients + shadow edges - tolerance absorbs software-rasterizer depth/float differences. Browser coverage joins when the lit web proof lands (src/render/web/README.md § follow-ups); web T1 equivalence is gated today by triangle3d + sprite2d plus the shared-WGSL construction.

viewport.renderer: viewport_scene.h::render_golden_viewport (the M5-F1 observer viewport composite: the 3D triangle base + the R-2D-001 sprites overlaid on top, one frame)
viewport.description: The M5-F1 observer-viewport composite (issue #164): the 'live scene (3D+2D)' the native viewport panel renders over context_render(_wgpu) composited via the L-41 seam - the T1 3D triangle (triangle3d) as the base layer with the T1 2D sprites (sprite2d) painted on top. Flat colors, opaque replace, no MSAA - tight tolerance. Native-only (like lit3d); web T1 equivalence is gated by the triangle3d + sprite2d browser goldens plus the shared-WGSL construction (the composite reuses those exact primitives, so equivalence within the T1 feature set follows). The committed baseline is the sprite-over-triangle-over-clear composite of the triangle3d + sprite2d goldens - byte-identical to `context_render_wgpu_offscreen golden viewport` (no blend / no MSAA), which the CI render job re-renders and SSIM-gates on every PR.

ui-hud.renderer: ui/hud_scene.h::render_golden_ui_hud (M7 a6 engine-integrated GPU UI backend, R-UI-005): the reference HUD (colored rects) authored as a retained UiTree, extracted through the GpuUiProvider and repainted into its persistent UI-layer texture over the T1 RHI
ui-hud.description: The M7 a6 UI HUD + a8 SHAPED TEXT (R-UI-005): a health bar (dark background + a green fill child painting on top), a blue minimap panel, a GPU-COMPOSITED amber badge (a scale-about-centre + translate applied at composite time, no relayout), a status bar - all solid opaque colored rectangles - PLUS a shaped-text label ('SCORE 1200', white, mid-left) added by M7 a8. Authored as an a1 UiTree, extracted to draw quads (snapshot.h), and repainted into the GpuUiProvider's persistent layer (provider.h) via the SAME ortho quad path (sprite/) the sprite2d golden already SSIM-gates; the a8 text is drawn on top as atlas-textured glyph CUTOUT quads (measure -> FreeType glyph atlas -> textured-glyph draw, the coverage thresholded at 0.5 with 1:1 integer pixel<->texel placement, so the GPU nearest-sample+discard and the analytic CPU mirror emit the SAME 1-bit glyph mask - the T1 RHI has no alpha blend, a cutout needs none). Flat colors + a 1-bit text mask, opaque replace, no blend / no MSAA - integer-aligned, so it rasterizes essentially identically native (lavapipe) and web (SwiftShader); tolerance 0.99. Rendered on BOTH backends (unlike native-only lit3d/viewport): the ui backend + the shaping text stack (FreeType/HarfBuzz) compile into the Emscripten web target too. The committed baseline is the analytic (GPU-free) rasterization of the SAME extracted quads + the 1-bit shaped-text mask (ui/hud_scene.h::render_ui_hud_reference_cpu), which the CI render + render-web jobs re-render on real adapters and SSIM-gate on every PR. Shaping CORRECTNESS (ligatures/marks/RTL/bidi/wrap) is proven by the headless ui-test_shaping / ui-test_line_break suites; this golden pins that shaped text reaches pixels.

ui-worldpanel.renderer: ui/worldpanel_scene.h::render_golden_worldpanel (M7 a9 world-space RTT UI panel, R-UI-003 / D4): a UiTree rendered into a dynamic-texture registry target (dynamic_texture.h) via the GpuUiProvider world-space form, then sampled onto a rotated world quad (a render::Transform-placed UiPanel) over a flat lit-ground base
ui-worldpanel.description: The M7 a9 world-space render-to-texture UI panel (R-UI-003; lock D4 dynamic-texture registry): a teal panel with an amber inner rect is rendered into a persistent 64x64 dynamic-texture registry target (the FIRST dynamic-texture entry - the 'later wave' the render_world.h texture handle fields reserved), then SAMPLED (nearest) onto a flat quad rotated (yaw + roll) / positioned by a render::Transform in a lit 3D scene - a receding-floor lit-ground trapezoid base under the panel, over a dark-slate sky clear. Straight-on ORTHOGRAPHIC projection (no perspective divide), so the rotated quad projects affinely and its UV interpolation is linear - the CPU mirror matches the GPU rasterizer within tolerance. Nearest sampling + flat colors + opaque replace / no blend / no MSAA. Native-only FIRST (like lit3d / viewport): the web golden target compiles only triangle3d + sprite2d today, so browser coverage joins when the lit web proof lands; native T1 equivalence is gated here. Tolerance 0.95 - a touch looser than ui-hud to absorb the rotated-quad edge coverage + the nearest-sampled texel boundaries a software rasterizer (lavapipe) resolves slightly differently from the analytic CPU baseline. The committed baseline is the analytic (GPU-free) rasterization of the SAME projected quads + panel content (ui/worldpanel_scene.h::render_worldpanel_reference_cpu), which the CI render job re-renders on the wgpu-native (lavapipe) backend and SSIM-gates on every PR.
